skill.name: deepchem
skill.description: Build molecular machine learning workflows with DeepChem for property prediction, MoleculeNet benchmarks, graph neural networks, and transfer learning.
skill.summary: Use DeepChem to prepare molecular data, train models, evaluate benchmarks, and predict chemical or biological properties.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many critical and high matches, but review found them to be documentation and scientific terminology false positives. The real concerns are limited to community Python scripts that read local datasets and may trigger DeepChem or HuggingFace downloads when users run them.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives from Markdown code examples and inline API names. The reviewed files contain DeepChem examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced Markdown examples or inline parameter names. No executable Ruby backtick syntax or shell command dispatch is present there.

low_findings.title: Windows SAM Alert Is a Genomics Format Reference
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM database finding is a false positive. The line names SAMLoader, BAMLoader, and CRAMLoader for biological sequence alignment formats.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a list of biological data loaders. There is no Windows path, registry access, credential extraction, or operating system interaction.

low_findings.title: C2 and Weak-Crypto Matches Are ML Terminology
low_findings.description: The C2 and weak cryptography alerts match common molecular ML terms, neural network layer names, dataset identifiers, and example SMILES strings. No command-and-control behavior or cryptographic operation is evident.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines define neural network layers, example molecules, and argparse choices. They do not contain sockets, beacons, encryption, hashing, or covert communication logic.

low_findings.title: Community Scripts Read Data and May Download Public Models
low_findings.description: The included Python scripts are legitimate ML utilities, but running them can read local CSV data and fetch public benchmark data or pretrained model assets through DeepChem or HuggingFace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicit and consistent with the skill purpose. The risk is operational rather than malicious, because no secret access or exfiltration path was found.

content.value_statement: Molecular machine learning requires careful data loading, featurization, splitting, and model selection. This skill guides DeepChem workflows for property prediction, benchmarks, graph models, and transfer learning.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use DeepChem for molecular, biological, and materials ML tasks.
Guides CSV, SDF, FASTA, and benchmark dataset loading with DeepChem loaders.
Compares fingerprints, descriptors, graph featurizers, and pretrained model featurizers.
Provides workflows for solubility, toxicity, binding affinity, and materials property prediction.
Includes scripts for solubility prediction, graph neural networks, and transfer learning.
Documents evaluation metrics, scaffold splitting, normalization, and common failure modes.
limitations: Users must install DeepChem and optional dependencies before running examples.
Some datasets and pretrained models may download external assets when scripts run.
Model quality depends on dataset size, labels, chemistry coverage, and validation design.
The skill provides workflow guidance, not regulatory or clinical validation.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Compounds
use_cases.description: Predict solubility, toxicity, or activity to rank candidate molecules before laboratory testing.

use_cases.title: Build Benchmark Models
use_cases.description: Train and compare DeepChem models on MoleculeNet datasets with scaffold-based evaluation.

use_cases.title: Fine-Tune Chemistry Models
use_cases.description: Adapt ChemBERTa or GROVER to small custom datasets for molecular property prediction.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Featurizer
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new molecular dataset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a DeepChem featurizer for a CSV dataset with SMILES strings and one solubility target. Explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Train a Baseline
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a first property prediction workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DeepChem workflow for solubility prediction using CircularFingerprint, ScaffoldSplitter, normalization, and regression metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Compare GNN Models
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate graph neural networks on a benchmark.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the DeepChem skill to compare GCN, GAT, and AttentiveFP on Tox21 with scaffold splits and ROC-AUC evaluation.

prompt_templates.title: Design Transfer Learning
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for limited labeled data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a ChemBERTa transfer learning experiment for a small custom activity dataset. Include split strategy, metrics, overfitting controls, and expected risks.

output_examples.input: I have 800 molecules with SMILES and solubility labels. What should I try first?
output: Start with CircularFingerprint and a Random Forest or MultitaskRegressor baseline.
Use ScaffoldSplitter to reduce leakage from related structures.
Report MAE, RMSE, and R squared on validation and test sets.

output_examples.input: Which DeepChem dataset should I use for toxicity classification?
output: Use Tox21 for multi-task toxicity classification benchmarks.
Use GraphConv or ECFP features depending on model complexity.
Evaluate with ROC-AUC and inspect class imbalance across tasks.

output_examples.input: My GNN performs worse than fingerprints. What should I check?
output: Confirm the dataset is large enough for graph models.
Check scaffold split difficulty and label noise.
Try AttentiveFP, DMPNN, or a pretrained model before increasing architecture size.

best_practices: Use scaffold splitting for molecular datasets to reduce structural leakage.
Start with fingerprint baselines before training larger graph neural networks.
Track validation metrics, class balance, and failed molecule featurization rates.
anti_patterns: Using random splits for drug discovery claims without checking scaffold leakage.
Training large graph models on very small labeled datasets without regularization.
Comparing models across different featurizers or splits without documenting the setup.

faq.question: What is DeepChem used for?
faq.answer: DeepChem supports machine learning for chemistry, biology, and materials science, including molecular property prediction and benchmark evaluation.

faq.question: Does this skill run models automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and example scripts. Users decide when to run scripts in their own environment.

faq.question: Which splitter should I use for molecules?
faq.answer: Use ScaffoldSplitter for most molecular property tasks to reduce leakage from similar structures.

faq.question: When should I use transfer learning?
faq.answer: Use transfer learning when labeled data is limited or when pretrained molecular representations fit the task.

faq.question: Can I use custom CSV data?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples support custom CSV files with SMILES columns and one or more target columns.

faq.question: Why did security review flag this skill?
faq.answer: Static analysis matched Markdown code fences and chemistry terms. Manual review found no malicious behavior.
